Wiki stored_url resolves to the same entity. Extract: 'Willie James "Blood" Ulmer (February 8, 1940 - June 3, 2026)' confirms birthYear 1940 and deathYear 2026. Guitarist and singer (instruments guitar, vocals match). Bio's Ornette Coleman connection confirmed ('the first electric guitarist to record and tour extensively with Coleman'); albums 'Tales of Captain Black' (1979) and 'Are You Glad to Be in America?' (1980) both in discography. 'Harmolodic theory' framing is editorial/consistent (discography includes 'Harmolodic Guitar with Strings').

Wiki stored_url resolves to the same entity. Extract confirms 'free jazz trio consisting of violinist Leroy Jenkins, bassist Sirone and percussionist/pianist Jerome Cooper', 'active from 1970-1977'. birthYear 1970 and deathYear null consistent (Jenkins's 2007 death precluded further reunions, but a group's end-year is not stated as a deathYear claim). Instruments piano/drums/bass/violin all represented. Bio's 'without a chordal instrument' describes the core violin-bass-drums lineup; Wiki notes piano was a secondary doubling instrument for Cooper, so this is an editorial characterization of primary instrumentation, not a flaggable contradiction. Cooperative/democratic framing matches Wiki.

Wiki stored_url resolves to the same entity. Founded 1977 (matches birthYear 1977); original members Julius Hemphill, Oliver Lake, Hamiet Bluiett, David Murray all confirmed; instruments alto/tenor/baritone/soprano sax consistent ('without a rhythm section' matches Wiki's 'principally recorded and performed as a saxophone quartet'). UNVERIFIABLE claim: bio cites "'Revue' album is a landmark of collective improvisation" but the Wiki Albums section is empty in the extract and 'Revue' is not named among any listed albums (W.S.Q., Live in Zurich, Dances and Ballads, Steppenwolf, Yes We Can) - the specific 'Revue' album cannot be checked against available evidence. Not contradicted, so unverifiable rather than factual_error. Note also: Wiki says the ensemble split up in 2016; deathYear is null (an omission, not a contradiction since ours makes no still-active claim).
